title: TypeScript Segment 01
summary: The earliest segment of TypeScript compiler development covering 409 PRs across 47 dependency layers. This segment spans the foundational build-out of the TypeScript language service, including the core compiler pipeline (parser, binder, checker, emitter), the services layer providing IDE features (syntax classification, formatting, completions, quick info), the test harness, and extensive test baselines. It represents the initial construction of TypeScript's language tooling infrastructure.
instructionPreview: `/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.
instruction:
You are an autonomous engineer. The repository at /workspace is at an earlier state of a real-world project.

`/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.

moduleDag.description: The earliest segment of TypeScript compiler development covering 409 PRs across 47 dependency layers. This segment spans the foundational build-out of the TypeScript language service, including the core compiler pipeline (parser, binder, checker, emitter), the services layer providing IDE features (syntax classification, formatting, completions, quick info), the test harness, and extensive test baselines. It represents the initial construction of TypeScript's language tooling infrastructure.

nodes.label: Core Compiler
nodes.description: The core TypeScript compiler including the parser, scanner, binder, type checker, and emitter. Handles type resolution, overload resolution, generic inference, and JavaScript code generation. Touched by 80 PRs in this segment.

nodes.label: Syntax Services
nodes.description: The syntax tree layer providing incremental parsing, syntax classification, and abstract syntax tree representations. Contains the incremental parser and syntax node definitions used by all IDE features.

nodes.label: Services Compiler Bridge
nodes.description: Bridge layer between the core compiler and the language service, providing diagnostic reporting, symbol resolution, and type information to IDE features.

nodes.label: Formatting Service
nodes.description: Code formatting engine providing auto-indentation, brace formatting, and configurable formatting rules for the TypeScript language service.

nodes.label: Services Core
nodes.description: Core language service infrastructure including the LanguageService interface, host abstractions, and shared service utilities for completions, diagnostics, and navigation.

nodes.label: Text Utilities
nodes.description: Text manipulation utilities including line/column mapping, text change tracking, and source text abstractions used across the services layer.

nodes.label: Service Resources
nodes.description: Localized string resources and diagnostic message definitions used by the language service for error messages and UI strings.

nodes.label: Language Service Features
nodes.description: Higher-level language service features including completions, go-to-definition, find-all-references, rename, signature help, and other IDE interactions built on top of the syntax and compiler bridge layers.

nodes.label: Test Harness
nodes.description: Test infrastructure providing compiler host mocking, baseline comparison, fourslash test execution, and test runner utilities for the TypeScript test suite.

nodes.label: Compiler Binaries
nodes.description: Entry-point scripts and compiled output for tsc (the command-line compiler) and the language service server, including bundled JavaScript output.

nodes.label: Build Scripts
nodes.description: Build and development scripts for compiling the TypeScript compiler itself, running tests, and managing the development workflow.

nodes.label: Library Definitions
nodes.description: Built-in type definition files (lib.d.ts) providing type information for the JavaScript standard library (DOM, ES5, ES6 APIs).

nodes.label: Documentation
nodes.description: Language specification documents and developer documentation describing TypeScript's type system, grammar, and compiler architecture.

nodes.label: Test Baselines
nodes.description: Expected output baselines for compiler and services tests, including emitted JavaScript, type errors, symbol tables, and project build outputs.

nodes.label: Compiler Test Cases
nodes.description: Test input files exercising compiler features like type inference, generics, modules, overloads, and error recovery scenarios.

nodes.label: Conformance Tests
nodes.description: ECMAScript conformance test cases verifying scanner behavior, parser correctness for strict mode, tuple types, type aliases, and statement parsing.

nodes.label: Fourslash Tests
nodes.description: Interactive editor feature tests using the fourslash test format to verify completions, go-to-definition, formatting, and other IDE behaviors.

nodes.label: Legacy Fourslash Tests
nodes.description: Older fourslash test cases migrated from the initial language service implementation, covering a broad range of editor scenarios.

edges.label: services compiler bridge wraps and exposes core compiler APIs
syntax layer shares parser primitives with core compiler
compiler bridge uses syntax tree for type-aware features
formatting rules operate on syntax tree nodes
language service features build on core service infrastructure
IDE features use compiler bridge for type information
syntax layer uses text utilities for source positions
formatter uses text change tracking for edits
bin contains the compiled output of the compiler source
harness generates and compares test baseline outputs
compiler test cases exercise compiler features
fourslash tests verify language service feature behavior
legacy fourslash tests cover older service scenarios
compiler loads lib.d.ts for built-in type information
core services use localized resource strings
